⚡ Smart trainer mandatory

----------------

🇩🇪🇦🇹🇨🇭 INFORMATION ZUM RIDE

Kilometers. Lactate. Unity. Breakaways.
Das sind wir - die KLUB CYCLING COMMUNITY.

Tritt dem KLUB unter Clubs auf Zwift bei. 💟

Ein strukturierter Ride, der Ausdauer, optionales Racing und Taktik in einem Format vereint. Mit Lead im Ride versuchen wir mit dem Peloton eine Pace von 2 bis 3 Wkg zu rollen.

💬 Nutze die Chatfunktion für das maximale Socialride Erlebnis.

🔵 Kilometers
Jeder Ride umfasst mindestens 40 km.

🔴 Lactate
Alle Segmente (Sprints & KOMs) und das Finale über die Ziellinie sind offen für alle die, die Bock drauf haben. Ganz und gar wie die Ortsschildsprint draußen. 😎 Ihr kennt es: #eseskalierteh 🔥

🟢 Unity
Schnelle Zeiten erfordern Teamwork. 💨 Gruppe finden 💨 Zusammen arbeiten 💨 Clever rollen, nicht allein

⚙️ ZWIFTPOWER VORAUSSETZUNGEN

⚡ Smarttrainer Pflicht

----------------

✅ JOIN our KLUB CYCLING COMMUNITY

🫶🏼 No Hate.
🫶🏼 No Ressentiment.

🌍 Who we are: We are you! We are everyone! Together we are your cross-team community, regardless of whether you already belong to a club or not. A community for all Zwifters, primarily from the DACH region, but open for the lovely world. No competition, no hate, no resentment. Instead, fun, motivation and healthy, sportingly fair ambition. We welcome all newcomers to the racing and event scene, help and share insights with each other. Go for it, we look forward to seeing you!

name: Team CLS Ride n Explore
description:
Come and join Team CLS for a mid-week ride. Clip in and relax as we ride at a steady C Cat pace with plenty of chat – Don't expect all weeks to be easy, some courses have a lot of climbing but we will support each other and get through them.

This is a chance to enjoy a friendly experienced midweek group ride with regulars who have no Big Egos. This one should definitely be in your weekly schedule as it adds valuable endurance km's at the same time that will improve your fitness.

PACE
After an easy roll out, we will settle into a relaxed pace of 2.5 on the flats and around 3.0 on the climbs, however short ramps may require some increased effort just like IRL. 

It is impossible to find the perfect pace to suit all on a ride like this, and as the leader can't slow repeatedly at the expense of others and a few dropped riders are inevitable, so we apologise in advance. One thing we can assure you we will not chase or talk about flyers.

Team CLS have some awesome leaders, male and female, whose job it is to keep you entertained throughout so they will always be throwing things into the mix on the ride.

You will probably be accompanied by members of our famous ‘ORANGE ARMY’, members of TEAM CLS CC who are dedicated to their vocation of helping others. If you have had a drop out or accidently hit a wrong key, type ‘HELP’ and they will come back and offer you a wheel back to the group.


TEAM CLS LINKS
Check out www.teamclscc.co.uk ...find out about our club, current events and leaders. 

Facebook Club Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclscc
Facebook Racing Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclsracing
Zwift Power Team - https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=1426

WEEKLY PROGRAM
Mon  18:50 UK - Team CLS Racing - Pace ‘n’ Race: Recon and Activation 
Tues 18:50 UK - This ‘n’ That - Endurance Training
Wed  18:50 UK - Ride ‘n’ Explore Club Rideout
Thur 18:50 UK - High ‘n’ Low - Interval Training
Sat  08:50 and 14:45 UK - Base ‘n’ Banter Endurance ride
Sun  18:50 UK - Chill 'n' Spin - Weekend Social


TEAM | CLS - Training | Racing | Community

name: RollCo: Rolling Tide - 12K
description:
🌊 ROLLING TIDE
Start small. Build big.
Welcome to Rolling Tide — RollCo’s season-long ritual race.

Every week, the distance grows by 1 km.
It begins as a short, explosive opener and steadily evolves into a full-distance endurance epic by year’s end.
This isn’t a one-off event — it’s a shared journey that rises with you.

🏁 Race Format
Rolling Tide is a categorised scratch race.
All riders start together, with categories keeping the racing fair and fast.
The only thing that changes… is the distance.

Week 1 → 1 km
Week 52 → 52 km

Early weeks reward raw power.
Later weeks demand patience, grit, and endurance.
The tide never stops rising.

Rolling Tide is about more than today’s finish line.
It’s about showing up — week after week.
Seeing the same names.
Measuring how far you’ve come as the distance stretches.

You’ll build rivalries.
You’ll track your progress.
You’ll remember the day it all began.

And by the end of the season, everyone who stayed will share the same truth:

“We rode the whole tide together.”

Why Join Rolling Collective?
We’re more than a club — we’re a global community of riders who support each other, grow together, and celebrate every ride, whether it’s a grind, a badge hunt, or a race. Join us on Discord, connect with fellow riders, and be part of something bigger.

Forward, Together

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt13 BIKE
description: WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

name: Cheesecake Crew - Chase the Cake
description:
Looking for something to help tackle the mid-week blues? Why not come and join the Cheesecake Crew for a fun-filled ride with a little bit of something for everyone.
We’ll cruise round together at a steady 2.7-3.0wkg before the start of our optional race ending – the Cake Chase!
Don’t want to race this week, we’ve got you covered. Our Yellow beacon will continue to cruise on home at the advertised pace.
Race or pace? The choice is yours. Whatever you choose you’ll be guaranteed a warm welcome and good old chin wag.

name: Cycling Weekly Club 10
description:
The evening ten is a staple of the British club scene as riders up and down the country will compete with their club mates midweek on a local course. Typically run following the post rush hour traffic the evening ten is as social as it is competitive as road riders, time trailer and club-men come together to compete, chase kudos and bragging rights.
 
About this race
Join staff members of Cycling Weekly, multiple time trial national champion Dr Hutch and a few special guests for a classic, ten mile time trial effort. Using a variety of courses on the Zwift platform the CW club ten will be a test of your power and your pacing. Results will feature on Zwift Power and in Cycling Weekly magazine each week alongside a profile of a competitor.
 
How to ride a TT
As the courses vary, so will your pacing. The flattest courses require a steady sustained effort. With the effort lasting anywhere between 20 and 30 minutes depending on your speed, you will need to know what power you can sustain for that amount of time, getting you across the line with little left in your legs. Finish too fresh and you haven’t pushed hard enough, but if you start slowing before you cross the line, you’ve gone out too hard.
 
When on the hillier courses pacing strategy becomes even more important. Time can be gained on rivals by pushing on the ascents and recovering on the descents, just don’t back off too much. But remember, if there’s a harder climb near the end, you’ll need to hold a little back to maintain your power output in the final push.
 
Your aim should be to ‘get it all out’. If you do this you’ll know you couldn’t have gone any faster. Come back each week and you’ll see an improvement, and be ranked in the series on Zwift Power.
 
How to join the event
Join via the Zwift companion app and be pedalling in a Zwift world well ahead of the event start time. You’ll be given a prompt to join the event in the bottom left of the screen. Do this and you’ll be taken to the start and placed on a conveyor belt where you can keep spinning your legs until you get to the front of the queue. When the time comes your avatar will speed up to 20 miles per hour for a rolling start.
 
About Dr Michael Hutchinson
Ten time winner of the national ten, six time winner of the national 25 and thirteen time winner of the national 50. Dr Hutch is also a best selling author having written about his attempt at the Hour Record and his thirst for going ever faster. He works as an aerodynamic consultant to Team Ireland and writes a weekly column in Cycling Weekly magazine
 
About Cycling Weekly
Established in 1891 Cycling Weekly is the oldest cycling magazine in the world and the only weekly publication. Covering international and domestic racing, the magazine also covers health, fitness and nutrition and reviews of the latest tech.

#cyclingweekly #club10

name: Cycling Ireland Esports Social Ride
description:
Welcome to the Cycling Ireland Social Ride.
The aim for this ride is to keep everyone together and have fun and ride at a steady pace of around 1.8-2.2 w/kg and we might include some efforts, but we will allow enough recovery and time to regroup.
This is NOT A RACE , but a social activity which will help you to gain some skills, knowledge and confidence. Each spin will have a fence to help to control the pace, your co-operation is therefore essential to the success of the social ride. If you are feeling strong, we would welcome your assistance on bringing back riders that might be struggling behind.

The ride will last for 60 minutes, however if you can’t stay that long, join the fun for as long as you like! Late-join also available.

TT Bikes don’t draft and requires pushing harder to pace within a group. Also, other riders cannot help you back to the group if you fall behind. To help avoid these issues we recommend not using a TT Bikes on these rides.
Have fun and enjoy!

name: TUZ: Spring Series Group Ride
description:
Last few weeks of TUZ events before we close for the summer..
This week we are offering two rides over Watopia Seaside sprint x 6 laps
Cat C: aimed @ Cat A - C
Cat E: aimed @ Cat D

Ross Blayney will be leading the Group C event
Group E:
The "Alternative Ulster" Cat E ride will be led by Stephen Cardy
Everyone is welcome, it is a social ride aimed at <2.0 w/kg :) so stick with the leader on this one or join the Cat C event.

Cats & Start Times:
19:00: C - TUZ: Spring Series Group Ride & After Party
19:00: E - TUZ: Alternative Ulster Social Ride

Heart Rate Monitors must be worn no ZP in results

Results will be posted on the TUZ Facegroup page & Zwiftpower

Remember to sign up to Zwiftpower & the TUZ Team @https://zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=2035

Who are Team Ulster Zwifter's (TUZ)?
TUZ was founded by Ross Blayney to enable riders from cycle clubs throughout the province of Ulster (Ireland) to race and train together.
TUZ are simply a group of local Ulster riders & guests with no affiliation to any official organisation, club or body.

Objective:
TUZ's aim is to provide a platform for local cycling clubs mainly but not exclusively from the province of Ulster to train & compete on the Zwift platform.
We make no apologies for targeting local riders and clubs. We feel this adds to the experience and as riders are known to each other (many have raced IRL together or simply known by name or reputation on Strava). This invariably builds in a level of integrity to often inaccurate Zwift power figures.
A dedicated Strava Group has been created where riders can compare local segment times and power figures.
The events are not exclusive to TUZ members and we welcome riders from the international community, just be prepared for a bit of friendly banter if you choose to sandbag one of our events.


More information, links and news about the TUZ & the TUZ Galibier Winter Racing League - can be found below:

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Inox Master Phenotypes Cup ⛰️ Climb 2/3
description:
3 short races, flat-climb-TT, to discover your racing phenotype, with categories based on age (Cat. A=up to 29 years, B=30–39, C=40–49, D=50–59, E=60+):
are you more of a sprinter, a climber, or a time trialist?

The races take place 20 minutes apart, making this format ideal for high-intensity efforts with recovery in between, perfect both for racing and structured training.

Each race focuses on a different specialty:

Race 1 – Sprint: a flat course, ideal for pure sprinters
Race 2 – Climb: a hilly route, designed for climbers
Race 3 – Time Trial: an individual time trial, in which riders will use an advanced time trial bike, identical for all, provided automatically.

As mentioned above, this event uses categories based on age, not power:

Cat A (Elite) → up to 29 years old
Cat B (M30) → 30–39 years
Cat C (M40) → 40–49 years
Cat D (M50) → 50–59 years
Cat E (M60) → 60+ years

At the end of the series, an overall classification on ZwiftPower will identify the most complete rider, based on performance across all three race types.

Each race awards points toward the overall classification:
50 points for 1st place, 49 points for 2nd place, 48 points for 3rd place and so on

The final overall standings after all three races will be available in the following league:
https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=3096

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: WOW Course Time Attack Race on Cadex TT bike with Draft
description:
The WOW Zwift events are the fastest way to collect happy miles while meeting exceptional riders/ friends on ZWIFT. While you will be assigned the fastest TT bike (Cadex) and the latest DT Swiss Disc wheel set in draft mode, you will still be upgrading your bike of choice selected in your garage before starting the ride.

Our Ds leave the pen first and hold the group together within D power limits. Several minutes later C’s will start and keep a higher end C pace. Further several minutes later the A-B train starts unleashing their magic to make up the delay from the groups starting ahead. Can all 3 teams come together in the final segments of the ride and celebrate the ONE WOW FAMILY? All groups will see each other, transfers between the groups on the go will be possible. The ride leaders will help make those transitions smooth.

WOW is a no drama, full fun ride!

Double draft is on and the Cadex TT bikes will be drafting!

name: Bioracer sprint series p/b Velos
description:
Bioracer Sprint Series p/b Velos

Welcome to the Bioracer Sprint Series, presented by Team Velos. This is a friendly social ride, but with a competitive edge: each month we change courses and have some fun sprinting. Novices are especially welcome, this is a great place to improve your sprint! Every week the top ten men and ladies are awarded points, 10 for the fastest sprint down to 1 point for 10th fastest. At the end of the month points are totaled to determine our Sprint Champions, who receive some Velos swag. (See rules below.)

We roll as a group at an average pace of 22mph/35kph (1.9w/kg). Our sweeps will support all riders struggling at the back, and we try our utmost to leave nobody behind: regrouping after every sprint.

New in September: a gift voucher for fantastic cycling kit from Bioracer Speedwear🇧🇪🎉 Thanks to our sponsor, we now offer a prize of €125 for each month’s top male and female sprinters.

RULES

1) Must have a Zwiftpower account. Points are only awarded to riders whose activity appears in the event on Zwiftpower.
2) Must wear a heart rate monitor.
3) To be eligible for prizes, winners must have been a member of Team Velos social groups (either on facebook at ⁦teamvelos.co.uk⁩, or the Velos club on Companion app) for a minimum of 1 month before the start of the series competing in.
4) Only one €125 prize will be awarded to a rider in a 6 month period.
5) An additional 5 points will be awarded to riders completing the ride every week of the month (loyalty points).
6) To promote opportunities for more winners, the previous month's champions are awarded half points for 3 months.

Join us on facebook at: ⁦teamvelos.co.uk⁩

Bioracer store:
⁦https://shop.bioracer.com/pages/about-bioracer-1⁩

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit || Advanced
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Neon After Party
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Inox Master Phenotypes Cup ⏱️ TT 3/3
description:
3 short races, flat-climb-TT, to discover your racing phenotype, with categories based on age (Cat. A=up to 29 years, B=30–39, C=40–49, D=50–59, E=60+):
are you more of a sprinter, a climber, or a time trialist?

The races take place 20 minutes apart, making this format ideal for high-intensity efforts with recovery in between, perfect both for racing and structured training.

Each race focuses on a different specialty:

Race 1 – Sprint: a flat course, ideal for pure sprinters
Race 2 – Climb: a hilly route, designed for climbers
Race 3 – Time Trial: an individual time trial, in which riders will use an advanced time trial bike, identical for all, provided automatically.

As mentioned above, this event uses categories based on age, not power:

Cat A (Elite) → up to 29 years old
Cat B (M30) → 30–39 years
Cat C (M40) → 40–49 years
Cat D (M50) → 50–59 years
Cat E (M60) → 60+ years

At the end of the series, an overall classification on ZwiftPower will identify the most complete rider, based on performance across all three race types.

Each race awards points toward the overall classification:
50 points for 1st place, 49 points for 2nd place, 48 points for 3rd place and so on

The final overall standings after all three races will be available in the following league:
https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=3096

CONTACT US
• Website: https://www.teaminox.it/
• Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/inoxteam/
• ZwiftPower: https://zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=16461
• Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cpr3rBGaZy

name: SNOW CC Points Race League
description:
SNOW CC runs this weekly Zwift Points Race League. Each race offers 175 points for each race, 100 points for the finish, and 75 points for the primes. The series is 10 races and your best 5 count. Do them all, or pick your favorite course. All points are First Across the Line, including the finish. We publish the results and the league on Zwift Power, and we announce each race course and the points scoring on our Facebook Page on a Thursday.

Please see our Facebook page for more information about weekly races and special events:

https://www.facebook.com/SNOWCCRacingLeague

Race results are published on Zwift Power. You must be a member of ZwiftPower to score points.
See https://zwiftpower.com/ for details.

SNOW CC is a Zwift Race Team dedicated to fair and transparent racing on Zwift. We run a number of events on Zwift and welcome new members who are committed to maintaining the highest standards in Zwift Racing

name: Int Women Cycling AMS BETTER THAN CHOCOLATE'
description:
Do you want to get even StrongHer? Leave the sofa for one hour & join us for an effective interval work out. We are the cycling ladies from the flatlands (the Netherlands, that country with all those road bike champs ,-)) and we gather on Mondays and Wednesdays at 20h CET for a good hour of fun intervals. Here you train on your OWN level. We stick together as a group, so no need to worry you won't be able to keep up!! We also make a special playlist for each training. 

We ride with an additional playlist. You can look up the playlist on SPOTIFY by following Floortje Baas, the playlist will have the name of the workout: titled SPINNING IS WINNING

SEE YOU TONIGHT! XOXO 

INSTAGRAM: Int. Women's Cycling AMS. (https://www.instagram.com/invites/contact/?i=kvgx5dkb099&utm_content=3a7uf1r 
FACEBOOK: Int. Women's Cycling AMS

name: Crit Cade Steering Race
description:
Steering Required: This event is designed to maximize the fun of Zwift Steering! If you have a steering device, now's the time to master those apexes and gain an advantage. If you do not have a steering device, unfortunately you will not be able to join.

Get ready for an electrifying dash under the lights in the Crit Cade Steering Racing Experience! This isn't your average crit—it's 8 laps of pure, high-octane racing Downtown Dolphin circuit, but with a thrilling arcade twist!

Power Up Pandemonium: This race features Power Ups! Strategically deploy your Boost Power ups to cut through the field. Timing is everything! Each Boost power up will give you a 10kph speed increase for 2 seconds!

Speed Boosts Galore: Keep your eyes peeled for additional Speed Boosts scattered across the course to give you that extra injection of pace when you need it most. But watch out because these boosts can move around, so you’ll need to be on top of your steering game!

Steering Required: This event is designed to maximize the fun of Zwift Steering! If you have a steering device, it’s the time to master those apexes and gain an advantage.

These events are held within our Zwift Labs club. This feature is still in development and we'd love your feedback on your experience.

name: PTz Workout By EnduranceLab.ie - VO2max Intervals Ride
description:
VO2max Intervals Rides are designed to increase aerobic capacity, or the ability to use oxygen to power your cycling. Aim to complete each 2-minute Interval at an output you could sustain for 6 minutes in race circumstances, which equates to an initial RPE of 7-8 on a 1-10 scale.
Try to maintain a steady output from the start of the first interval to the end of the last interval. Also pay attention to your output in the Zone 1 recovery segments. If you find yourself slowing down in these segments as the workout progresses, you may be doing the Zone 4 intervals too hard.
Feel free to do your Zone 4 efforts uphill, especially if you're training for a hilly event.
Join our club:
Zwift: https://www.zwift.com/eu/clubs/ptz/home
Zwiftpower: https://zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=1
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ptzprivatefunfast
Workout powered by EnduranceLab:
https://www.endurancelab.ie/

The series will also be found on zwiftpower where you can track your progress against your rivals.

name: EVO CC Sprint Race Series
description:
Welcome to EVO sprintrace series. Shorter more compact races for the rider looking for an effective high intensity race. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: Giant Camden Triathlon Endurance 6
description: This is the sixth endurance session aimed at triathlon endurance, or cycling endurance generally. The sessions are focused around L2-3 work, with some variations in intensity

name: Flanders Cycling Happening
description:
Join us Flandriens at this moderate paced group ride with fast finishes ! 
We start the ride easy at 2.0 w/kg and get up to speed at 2.5 w/kg in group. This is a social group ride with chats and fun, and a mini-race in the last couple of kilometers of the course. 
Every week we pick another route, both flat as well as climbing routes to keep you motivated. Wait for the signal of the leader to start your fast finish. Results will be displayed when you cross the finish line. Please follow the leader’s instructions to make it fun for all the riders ! Need a hand in the back, then you can rely on our sweeper’s help. Late joining is possible in the first half an hour after the start of the event. 

#BZRcc 

Join us in our Belgian community: https://www.facebook.com/groups/bzrecyclingclubhttps://www.facebook.com/BZRECYCLINGTV

eventSubgroups.name: Flanders Cycling Happening (C)
eventSubgroups.description:
Join us Flandriens at this moderate paced group ride. It’s a 2-2.5 w/kg group ride, it’s not a race. OK, there is a fun race part at the end called the "FESTIVAL FLANDRIEN". Wait for the ride leader to announce when it starts. Results will be displayed when you cross the finish line.
Please follow the LEADER’s instructions to make it pleasant for all the participants !
When you start the Festival in front of the leader you'll be DQ'd.
If you're an obvious flier the BZR team will flag you !
Late joining is possible ...
#BZRcc #bzr_spsd
Our BZR stream Channels :
https://www.twitch.tv/tsbiker
https://www.youtube.com/user/TSbiker10
https://www.facebook.com/BZRECYCLINGTV
Our BZR Spotify playlist : BZR.cc FTP motivator

name: 3R Launchpad Ride [~1.0-1.5w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise

name: The Knights Race Series - Series XI
description:
Wednesday nights are race Knights! The Knights Race Series is
a weekly race designed to fit in with working Zwifters who need a
conveniently timed event that doesn't compromise the rest of their weekly
training.The series is split into 6 Stages. Riders are awarded points
depending on their finish position in a stage, split by category. 7 points
for a win, 6 for 2nd, 5 for 3rd and so on until 1 point for 7th place.
We're also introducing SPRINT sections for each stage with 3/2/1 points
awarded to the three fastest on the Sprint segment across all
categories. As the dust settles on Series XI, we are running an exhibition race on AdZ - perfect for those hunting a PB on the mountain .BKRS offers punchy, frenetic racing that totals no more
than one hour, regardless of course unless we take on the Alpe or Ven-Top.
Please note, if you are competing using zPower or without a HR monitor, you
are welcome to join in but will not be eligible for points classification.
All results will go off Zwiftpower - please enter the correct cat!
To stay
up to date with standings and other news - please join the Strava/Facebook
Club:https://www.facebook.com/groups/theknightsraceseries/
https://www.facebook.com/groups/theknightsraceseries/

It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

name: PACK Run: Step by Step + After Party
description:
New to running, coming back after some time off, or simply want a nice warm up or cool down session? Join us as we move the legs in this slow-paced, social event. The goal of the event is to encourage folks to move, but we have also added some hills for the 8k distance. Not feeling the hills? Join the 5k category and simply tick off the kilometers (or miles). If you do decide to add in some hill work, you will be prompted along the way to change your incline, so all you have to do is focus on running.
The leader will average 8kph (7:30min/km) // 5mph (12min/mile) for the duration. While we encourage runners to stick with the leader, feel free to go at your own pace.

Put your black and orange tops and socks on and let's get moving!

This series of points races lasts for 6 weeks and takes place every Wednesday night at 20.00 CET.

For each race there is a result per category and a general classification.

name: VAN RYSEL GROUP RIDE
description:
Welcome to the Van Rysel exit it will be a friendly outing in
in order to build our community as we go. Every week you can participate
in an outing with a different route and varying difficulty. Only one
group leader with whom you can interact and who will help you acquire
a better level during the winter. You can speed up for
segments, then regroup to stay in a group
until the last final sprint.

WE STAY GROUPED UNTIL THE CLIMB

Van Rysel is a French brand and a community where everyone is welcome.
Find us on Facebook.
-> VAN RYSEL ZWIFT RACING CLUB

Discord: https://discord.gg/EmPjyr2u67

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: ART Jersey Unlock Double Draft Race
description:
Please join Atom Racing Team for a weekly double draft race where everyone wins and be the proud owner of the best in game kit in Zwift on completion (source not verified).
This is a category enforced event with #Doubledraft to try to encourage the group to work together. To further aid this we will try to only select flat courses.

Join our facebook group should you want to join the team for more events, look us up on Twitter or ask to join the Zwift club after the race. www.atomracingteam.co.uk

eventSubgroups.name: ART Jersey Unlock Double Draft Race (A)
ART Jersey Unlock Double Draft Race (B)
ART Jersey Unlock Double Draft Race (C)
ART Jersey Unlock Double Draft Race (D)
ART Jersey Unlock Double Draft Race

name: HISP- The Route of the Week
description:
A race with HISP plus some free XPs? Can’t get any better! Every week we’ll compete on the Route of the Week, with three time slots a day, all part of a monthly league (4 stages)
⚠️ IMPORTANT: to claim your XPs, make sure you’ve clicked on the Route of the Week banner on Zwift’s main page and joined the challenge previous to the Race.
The league’s overall standings will follow ZRS and will be available on ZwiftPower.


¿Una carrera con HISP más unos XPs Gratis? ¡Mejor imposible! Todas las semanas competiremos en la ruta de la semana, con tres horarios al día, en una liga mensual (4 etapas)
⚠️ IMPORTANTE: recuerda que para recibir los XP tienes que haber accedido al banner Ruta de la Semana en la página principal de Zwift para inscribirte al desafío antes de la carrera.
La clasificación general de la liga se regirá por ZRS y estará disponible en ZwiftPower.
shortDescription: Descubre cada semana tus limites en la alta montaña

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter
accessExpression:
(profile.playerType == "BROADCASTER") ||
(subgroup.label == 1 && scoring.current >= 390 && scoring.current <= 650) ||
(subgroup.label == 2 && scoring.current >= 270 && scoring.current < 510) ||
(subgroup.label == 3 && scoring.current >= 160 && scoring.current < 390) ||
(subgroup.label == 4 && scoring.current < 270) ||
(subgroup.label == 5 && scoring.current < 160)

name: 3R True2 Steady Ride [2.0w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack.  We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

#3r

name: TBR MounTRONeering
description:
TBR Mountroneering is your opportunity to climb Watopia’s biggest mountain, Alpe du Zwift, in a regular weekly group ride.

Our awesome team are here to help you reach new heights!

Key points
• Have fun, enjoy the ride and making new friends while suffering a little together
• Roll out at 2.5w/kg for 5km to the base of the climb
• Climb the 12.2km with 1036m of elevation at 2.9w/kg (sub 70 minute ascent)
• An optional Smasho race is often added from turn 4 to the finish line for those with fresh legs
• After the climb the Yellow beacon group will ‘drop like a rock’, your chance to break a PR and potentially hit 100km/hr
• This is a no fence ride, you won’t get heckled for riding at your own pace (whether faster or slower)

name: CMA Ladies Only Social Ride
description:
The focus for this group ride is to have fun! We are not just mums, but women who inspire, encourage, and empower each other. All women are welcome to join, especially those new to Zwift or wanting some good company to start off their week! The pace will be between 1.3-1.7wkg throughout the 45min duration. We hope to see you in Watopia! ABOUT THIS RIDE This ride is lead by Cycling Mums Australia. Our members all share a similar love for two wheels, they are mums, some are non mums, they know a mum, one day want to be a mum and dedicated to make cycling fun and safe for all and our future generations. So if you want to live a rich life with laughter, love and support, join us! http://www.cyclingmumsaustralia.com.au/ 

#cma

name: The Velos Klick - 1km Challenge
description: A klick is a military term for a distance of 1km, and so Team Velos brings you a challenge to complete 1km in the fastest time possible. We will change courses monthly (all Flat courses) and we will have winners from each category at the end of the month. Choose your bike very carefully as it could make all the difference, the challenge is available every night during the week use it as a warm up for a race or just use it to improve your 1km time or power figures, whatever just have fun.

name: Crit Cade Steering Race
description:
Steering Required: This event is designed to maximize the fun of Zwift Steering! If you have a steering device, now's the time to master those apexes and gain an advantage. If you do not have a steering device, unfortunately you will not be able to join.

Get ready for an electrifying dash under the lights in the Crit Cade Steering Racing Experience! This isn't your average crit—it's 8 laps of pure, high-octane racing Downtown Dolphin circuit, but with a thrilling arcade twist!

Power Up Pandemonium: This race features Power Ups! Strategically deploy your Boost Power ups to cut through the field. Timing is everything! Each Boost power up will give you a 10kph speed increase for 2 seconds!

Speed Boosts Galore: Keep your eyes peeled for additional Speed Boosts scattered across the course to give you that extra injection of pace when you need it most. But watch out because these boosts can move around, so you’ll need to be on top of your steering game!

Steering Required: This event is designed to maximize the fun of Zwift Steering! If you have a steering device, it’s the time to master those apexes and gain an advantage.

These events are held within our Zwift Labs club. This feature is still in development and we'd love your feedback on your experience.

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt14 RUN
description: WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

name: AHDR Bacon Rolls p/b JetBlack
description:
The best way to start (or end) your day! Come and roll with AHDR in a social bunch at around 2.2-2.5w/kg. Please note this is at the higher end of D grade pace so a great way to push yourself a little harder, or ease into the weekend.

Positioning close to our Beacon will bring you the best experience and draft. Fun, teamwork and respect for each other will always be the focus!

All welcome - late join available for first 30 mins. Smiles mandatory, snacks optional.

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt15 BIKE
description: WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

name: Righto Champ Riot p/b Team VTO
description:
Join Team VTO’s Ben Shepherd (shep_just_shep) and Ben Pratt (the_average_ironman) for a legendary 5km run in partnership with Righto Champ Sunglasses—because every champ deserves to look spec-tacular! 🕶️🏃

This isn’t just any run—it’s a chance to celebrate men’s mental health, share laughs, and break a sweat. Whether you’re fast, slow, or somewhere in between, this event is all about fun and connection. 🎉

🎁 Bonus: A few lucky runners will score stylish Righto Champ Sunglasses!

Lace up, bring your best dad jokes, and let’s run, laugh, and champion mental health together.

Why did the dad bring a ladder to the race?
Because he wanted to go to the next level! 😄

See you there—because nothing says self-care like running 5km and having a laugh! 💪👏

Team Zwift Tri Oz is the home of Multisport Athletes in Australia on @gozwift.

We value FUN , FRIENDSHIP & DETERMINATION !

For more information, please join our Zwift Club - Team VTO on Zwift Companion
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/151ca95c-f6de-4fe2-ae88-a769e367f439/home

Join the chat on FB;
https://www.facebook.com/groups/thenewzwifttrioz

eventSubgroups.name: Righto Champ Riot p/b Team VTO (E)

name: EVR Europe Race
description:
The EVR Race is intended to provide an entry level race on Mondays, Wednesdays and Fridays for A, B, C and D racers in four different timezones. All categories start together. You are encouraged to race with the fastest participants you can keep up with. 

Be prepared for fast starts and hard racing.

Results can be seen at zwiftpower.com and will be based on your category.

#evr

eventSubgroups.name: EVR Europe Race (A)
eventSubgroups.description: #doubledraft

name: ZLDR Run Like No One is Watching
description:
Join Zwift Long-Distance Runners (ZLDR) run club for a 30 minute run. Whether you want to push for a new personal best, use it as part of your weekly training program or just enjoy a sociable run this is suitable for you. Come and do an easy walk or a quick sprint, whatever suits your training for the day!

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn

#zldr_run

eventSubgroups.name: ZLDR Run Like No One is Watching (E)

name: Bicycling Australia & The Classics Weekly Training Ride
description:
Bicycling Australia’s Bowral Classic training ride is a weekly event
rolling out of Watopia at 6 pm Thursdays.

It’s a tempo training ride – of course, you are welcome to go as hard as
you like.

Visit www.bicyclingaustralia.com.au for the latest cycling-related news in
Australia.

Join, share & spread the word using the #BowralClassic hashtag or visit
www.bowralclassic.com.au for more information

#bicyclingaustralia

eventSubgroups.name: Bicycling Australia & The Classics Weekly Training Ride (C)

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: REVO Social SUB2
description: Join us for a 1.5-2 w/kg paced ride on a flat course. Let's stick together and show newer riders the art and benefit of drafting! We will not chase riders that go off the front, and the ride lead may use the fence to keep everyone together. If you're feeling strong, please use your abilities and drop back and help pull the slower riders back up to the pack if someone gets dropped. Complete this ride and unlock our Revolution Velo kit! Hosted by Revolution Velo Racing, a Zwift team for women. To hear about our upcoming rides, check out our Facebook Fans page at https://www.facebook.com/groups/RevoFans/

name: The QueenBee tHrIVE Ride
description:
The QueenBees are a group of women who share a love of cycling. Just as a hive works together to bring out the best in every member of the community, so do we. We strive to support, empower and encourage women to live their best lives, no matter where they are on their life, cycling or fitness journey. Join us for a social ride, all abilities welcome pace will be around 1.3 - 1.7wkg. During the ride try to stay with the yellow lead beacon and follow instructions from your ride leader.

Join our QueenBee hive here (ladies only): https://www.facebook.com/groups/218570110355444

name: Workout Wednesday Run
description:
Join the Zwift Runners for a group run workout that will be of benefit to all distance runners.

The speeds for each group are based on a threshold speed of A group 15 kph, B 13 kph, C 11 kph, D 9 kph. We will start the intervals at that speed but increase the speed of the intervals we get into the workout. We encourage runners to choose the group closest to the effort level you want to run at and to keep to the nominal speed for that group to maintain the group dynamic. However if these speeds are significantly too low or high for you then you can choose to run in the group that is closest and then adjust the target speed up to 10% up or down in the game or via the Companion app.

If you have any questions about this event or want to find out more about Zwift Running in general then please visit the Zwift Runners Facebook group or zwift.com/run #gozwiftrun #wow

name: ZLDR Decaf Wednesday's
description:
We will ride for 45min with no coffee stop (Decaf) 

Yellow beacon will aim for average 1.8 wkg.
Red beacon will aim for average 1.6 wkg. 
Feel free to ride with Yellow or Red. 

All rider's are encouraged to ride within the advertised pace and stick with the ride leader. 

Ride On!

name: AusCycling Talent Identification
description:
Your journey to the top step starts in your living room.

AusCycling invites you to test yourself against the best. The workout comprises three free ride efforts: 15 seconds, 3 minutes and 12 minutes designed to test your critical power. As with any test or race, ensure you are appropriately warmed up and motivated.

The test is short and sharp, requiring maximum commitment. Erg mode will be turned off during the intervals, you are on your own and its your time to shine.

As you fade through the efforts, use your gears to reduce the resistance and maintain your cadence. Aiming for 90-100rpm for the efforts. Finish the efforts completely spent, the key is to finish all the efforts regardless of how much your power fades or how hard it feels.

Saddle up and enjoy the ride!

 Target Audience;
 16-22yo with no high-performance sport background
 People with vision or physical impairment – any age

People with background in high-performance sport – any age
*If you are outside the ages of 16-22 and have a background in high performance sport or vision/physical impairment please visit https://www.auscycling.org.au/page/virtual-tid and contact us for more information.

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Restart Esports - Wednesday Worlds
description:
Restart Esports - instagram.com/restart.esports

1. Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) is mandatory for all participants.

2. Power Source is Mandatory for all racers. A smart trainer or smart bike should be your power source where possible. ZPower is not allowed. 

3. Dual Recording - Should always be done if you have the capability. All podium positions are expected to dual record or have at least have a recent dual recording history on their profile. Any rider posting high level performances is expected to validate their hardware with dual recordings as a minimum. 

Results WILL be amended (if required), in the interest of fair play, for all Restart Esports hosted events.

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Chat and roll p/b TBR
description:
Join the TBR crew and roll with Chris Hoiberg for a 45min 2.2 - 2.3 w/kg cruise and chat. Our yellow beacon will average around 40km/h (25mph), consistent with a category C flat ride, with a bit of variation to cater for the majority of riders. Our red beacon may create a second group in the last half of the ride and average around 38km/h (24mph) to help as many riders as possible to finish in a group.

The aim of the ride is to support those wanting to progress from sub 2w/kg rides to something a bit quicker as they improve their strength and fitness. Stronger riders are welcome and your assistance in the group will be appreciated as we will have sweepers working throughout the ride.

So come along at 8.15am AEST each Wednesday and see what makes TBR such a friendly bunch. You're all welcome and you'll have a mighty good time!

We don't use the fence for this ride. All riders should exercise self-control and respect the ride leader by riding with the yellow beacon at all times. Your co-operation helps us provide everyone with a fun, supportive and inclusive ride.

Follow TBR at thebigring.cc to learn more about our events, read ride reports and leave feedback.

name: 3R Endurance Steady Ride
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. 

This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page
https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ 

#team3r #ride3r
#rideraceraise.

#3r

name: Old Timers Tea Time Tour
description:
Old Dog Racing presents a series of shorter and easier races ideal for beginner and intermediate riders. It’s called the tea time tour but it could be breakfast, lunch, maybe even midnight snack wherever you are!  Absolutely everyone is welcome to join in, bring your friends and a nemesis or two!

Perhaps you are new; maybe the old power station isn’t delivering the watts anymore, or the knees are just a little too creaky, these races are for you!

Come and join the fun; A six week tour, two start times per week, Wednesdays 5:30 pm Eastern and  Fridays 5:30 pm Pacific, If you race both events in a week your best finish time will go towards your points total.

Races will be mass start and all riders visible, participants will be issued the Zwift Category kit as an aid to identify which category riders you are currently racing against.

The Categories are divided by age as shown below:
A Under 40 (red)  B 40-49 (green) C 50-59 (blue) D 60-69 (yellow) and E 70 and over. (purple)

If you haven’t tried age based (masters) racing before, they can be great fun, plenty of banter and friendly rivalry between the age groups!

Watch out for the olduns’ (yellow and purple)… They say you can’t teach an old dog new tricks, but what they don’t tell you is that they have a few stashed up their sleeves!

Power ups to help you along your way include: The van, the steamroller, the feather, and aero.

If you find yourself alone, enjoy the scenery and clock up another route badge.

Points awarded to each category in every race are: 25, 20,16,13,11,10,9,8,7,6 and 5 very generous participation points!! (You have to finish!). 

Highest accumulated points in each category wins the series.. you’re going to have to wear an HRM and have a Zwift Power account in order to be awarded points.

Earn more than 100 points and l’ll send you an Old Dog Racing Mug!*
The best race report of the series gets a mug too.* 

      *terms and conditions apply, go to Facebook page for details.


Join us on :
Zwift Companion https://www.zwift.com/clubs/0ad7655f-797b-4dc2-940d-b313fca0df0a/home
Facebook -    https://www.facebook.com/groups/3001328763388965 
or Strava  -  https://strava.app.link/7JqgUWGwu1b

WOOF!

name: PartyAnimal 80 (Wheelsky 3.5+)
description:
Meet the most notorious zwifter group: Party Animal 80. Hop on. Grab the wheels. You are welcome:)

We won't call out ""front easy"" or ""back push push"". Use your zwift etiquette.

B++ 80 minutes of various party forms (unexpected race pace, strava segment PR breaker, chase the ace, TTT, silent auction, birthday celebration, anything you can reinvent out of zwift box).

When we realize we can never be one of those pros, we party in Zwiftopia. But hey, It's ok to be the minority. Do you train hard or hardly train? Or, you just wanna look tough? Join us. Be there or be dry.

#PA80

description:
Are you looking for the perfect social ride after an epic weekend of riding, or maybe just looking for some motivation to get the week going?

Look no further, the #GiantWednesdayMotivation ride is here for you to kick off the week with your fellow Zwifters, along with live Q&A in the chat. Our ride leaders will do their best to keep the group together with a 2.5-2.7 w/kg effort.

Use #GiantWednesdayMotivation and tag @GiantBicyclesUSA on social media to let us know you're joining the ride!

#wednesdaymotivation

name: SWX Wednesday Tempo by Coach Cindy 🇺🇸🇨🇦🇧🇷
description:
Build strength and control every Wednesday with SWX Tempo — a steady endurance builder designed to grow your aerobic engine.
Crafted by Coach Cindy (Train Smart = Race Stronger), this session focuses on sustained tempo efforts to improve muscular endurance, pacing, and long-ride efficiency. Bring smooth cadence, mental focus, and your best zone 3 vibes!

• All levels welcome
• Stay-together mode enabled
• Late join allowed

Connect and learn more about who we are:
📸 Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/shark_worx_swx
🌐 Website: https://bit.ly/Visit_Coach_Cindy

name: Ravecovery
description:
Welcome to Team Rave

It’s time to heal those dancing injuries from all the raving or the sore muscles from all the racing.   Join Team RAVE for a recovery ride with the Rave USA crew.  This is not a race but feel free to challenge yourself or take it easy.

The yellow beacon will cruise at 1.8 w/kg - 2w/kg. The red beacon at 1.0 w/kg .
Find a group that suits you, blob up but above all, HAVE FUN! That’s all that matters
#teamrave - rave like there's no tomorrow!

name: Safe Roads with It Could Be Me
description:
We want to invite all interested in cycling safety and getting involved in their communities to join us.
All abilities are welcomed.
We want to strengthen our It Could Be Me community to help eradicate the current negative narrative between drivers and vulnerable users.

https://www.itcouldbeme.org

Join us on Discord for articles, updates and, community events - including our interactive group rides on Zwift. Follow the link and help us make roads safer together.

https://discord.gg/zMtetyx3P9

name: USAT Workout Wednesday
description: This USA Triathlon ride will work on speed, power and strength. The ride contains a series of short, upper threshold segments followed by a challenging low cadence build to simulate climbing. The workout ends with descending efforts while increasing cadence. This USAT workout was prepared by Chris Kaplanis, professional coach and co-founder of RTA Triathlon. www.RTAtri.com | Social: @RTAtriathlon

name: Ascenders Asia Morning Group Run
description:
Come and join the Ascenders as we put on our running shoes and run
daily with our friends too by starting our day in a healthy way. Research
shows that just 30 minutes of running can have huge benefits on your
short-term and long-term health. So we designed our running events to
motivate athletes to run 30 minutes each day to boost your metabolism and
use those calories. Running will also help you look good and feel stronger.

Our group run is designed with runners of all levels in mind and set the
pace range to be determined by our Zwifter Runners. So find a group pace
that matches how you feel each day and make those steps count for you.
Runners who went beyond the event duration will find that we have selected
courses that will help you earn the Route Badges when you run the full
distance.

So lace up those running shoes as everybody is welcome to join with our
friendly and motivating runners.

# <https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/> Join Ascenders on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
# <https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/> Ascenders Discord Channel:
https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

name: Group Run 5k
description:
DESCRIPTION

Join us for a 5k group run.   It's run-at-your-own-pace and all are welcome! 

Pick the closest pace group for you, but all groups will start together and you are free to fall back and run ahead. The pace groups designate average pace for the duration of the run. Most groups will start slower and finish faster than the indicated pace. Feel free to mix in some intervals and race your friends.

name: The HERD Wacky Wednesday
description:
Whoop whoop, it's Wednesday! Come join The Herd on a silly and social roll around Zwift's finest routes!

This ride is a great choice for beginner and intermediate riders, or anyone looking for a recovery. Expect a LOT of fun chat, games, and a few surprises.

We focus on sticking as a group, and honouring the advertised pace of 1.3-1.5 w/kg (about 105W). Please stay with the Yellow Beacon. Stronger riders are encouraged to help the Red Beacon sweep anyone struggling off the back.

For even more fun, connect to our Discord voice channel, where you can join the chat or just listen in: see www.bit.ly/HerdDiscord to set yourself up before the ride. And check out The Herd’s Facebook group and many other goodies at www.TheHerd.Club.

#herd

name: ZTBR Endurance Group Ride (E)
description:
ENGLISH:
ZTBR eCycling holds this easy-paced ride every Wednesday! ALL ARE WELCOME!

Audience: The tour is suitable for new riders, those recovering from injury or anyone looking for a fun and regenerative experience.
Pace: This tour focuses on 1.0-2.5 w/kg. Please respect the leader's (who will give directions in the Chat), pace and walk along for better dynamics. Groups at a slower pace than the leader can form,

Stronger riders are welcome to help sweep or act as riding companions for others. If you're having trouble, send a message in Chat to ask for help from the ride scan.

We have late ticket enabled. If you fall off early on, it might be easier to just get off the ride and get back on. Late join works for the first 30 minutes of the trip.

This tour is normally 90 minutes long, but sometimes it can be longer or shorter to cover a specific Zwift route.

Join us on Instagram @ztbr_ecycling [website] ztbr.com.br

#ztbr #z2

==============================================

PORTUGUÊS:
O ZTBR eCycling realizada todas as quartas-feiras este passeio de ritmo leve! TODOS SÃO BEM-VINDOS!

Público: O passeio é adequado para novos pilotos, aqueles que se recuperam de uma lesão ou qualquer pessoa que busca uma experiência divertida e regenerativa.
Ritmo: Este passeio tem como foco 1,0-2,5 w/kg. Por favor, respeite o ritmo do líder (que passará orientações no Chat), e ande junto para melhor dinâmica. Grupos em ritmo menor que o líder poderão se formar, 

Os pilotos mais fortes são bem-vindos para ajudar na varredura ou atuar como companheiros de passeio para os outros. Se você estiver com dificuldades, envie uma mensagem no Chat para solicitar ajuda da varredura de passeio.

Nós temos o ingresso tardio ativado. Se você cair no início, pode ser mais fácil simplesmente sair do passeio e voltar. Late join funciona nos primeiros 30 minutos da viagem.

Esse passeio normalmente dura 90 minutos, mas às vezes pode ser mais longo ou mais curto para cobrir uma rota específica do Zwift.

Junte-se a nós no Instagran @ztbr_ecycling [website] ztbr.com.br

#ztbr #z2

name: Rally Serie Argentina
description:
Join us for a hard race in France having fun while riding.
Unite a una carrera frenetica mientras nos divertimos pedaleando

name: Rhino Banded Hot Dog Social
description:
Rhino Hot Dog Social ride is the epitome of the traditional Wednesday night club run.
The mid week recovery ride to punctuate the races that bookend the week. A purely social ride that aims to cater for everyone, ride is banded so whatever your pace you will stay with the yellow beacon leader group. So come and join Rhino Racing for a virtual Hot Dog, enjoy the company and have a fun Wednesday ride out. It's all about the ride!!

name: Bikealicious Wednesday Worlds
description:
Originating with a group a Zwifters located near Boston, Massachusetts, Wednesday Worlds is a banded/stay together ride open to cyclists and triathletes of all abilities, with novice/intermediate/advanced participants especially welcome. As a stay together (Rubberband) ride, you never will get dropped, ( unless you stop pedaling) thanks to Zwift's banded group ride feature. Ride as hard or as easy as you like, without worry of disrupting the overall ride. The goal of this ride is to provide a supportive opportunity for Zwifters to get together to ride and (if desired) socialize. Courses will vary each week and each ride will run 60 or 90 minutes. If you are looking for a consistent weekly ride with all-around enthusiastic people who are interested in all aspects of cycling, this event might be for you!


Your ride leader has a 40+ year history of top level cycling competition on both the road and track. Additionally, he draws on many years of coaching regional and national level cyclists and his work on aerodynamic testing to provide additional support to ride participants. Questions during the ride are encouraged!

name: Major Taylor 1899 (MT1899) Whirlwind Wednesday
description:
Join us for the Major Taylor 1899 Whirlwind Wednesday!

The ride leader will hold ~2.0 w/kg on the flats for the first 50 minutes to keep the group together. In the final 10 minutes, the fence drops, time to PUSH THE PACE and see who can hang on! 

This is a non-banded ride, so maintaining the stated pace is key to staying with the beacon. Please respect group ride etiquette.

Why Major Taylor?

Marshall Walter Taylor, known as “Major Taylor”, was a trailblazing cycling legend. In 1899, he became the first U.S. born African American world champion in any sport, wining the 1-mile and 2-mile sprint world titles, along with multiple national championships. 

This ride is hosted by the Major Taylor 1899 (MT1899) community cycling clubs across the USA united to honor his legacy. The acronym after each ride leader’s name identifies the hosting club.

Want to ride IRL with a local Major Taylor club?
https://www.majortaylorassociation.org/friends.shtml

Fun FACT: One of Major Taylor’s nicknames was “The Worcester Whirlwind.”

During the months of May through October there may be rides without an assigned ride leader due to the outdoor riding season. We invite all to connect with us on the social media platforms below to stay in the know:

Discord
https://discord.gg/PwT4BgU7au

Facebook
https://www.facebook.com/groups/418937925806730/?ref=share

Strava
https://www.strava.com/clubs/mt1899

Zwift Power
https://zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=12949

As we say in the Zwift community, Ride On!

name: Workout Wednesday Run
description:
Join the Zwift Runners for a group run workout that will be of benefit to all distance runners.

The speeds for each group are based on a threshold speed of A group 15 kph, B 13 kph, C 11 kph, D 9 kph. We will start the intervals at that speed but increase the speed of the intervals we get into the workout. We encourage runners to choose the group closest to the effort level you want to run at and to keep to the nominal speed for that group to maintain the group dynamic. However if these speeds are significantly too low or high for you then you can choose to run in the group that is closest and then adjust the target speed up to 10% up or down in the game or via the Companion app.

If you have any questions about this event or want to find out more about Zwift Running in general then please visit the Zwift Runners Facebook group or zwift.com/run #gozwiftrun #wow

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Times Square Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Team I Race Like A Girl Workout
description:
We are in our build phase for a few weeks. Intensity will be added progressively with a variety of workouts targeting top end power. Our favorite high intensity intervals will also continue to make a regular comeback. Use these workouts as a smart addition to your weekly training plans!

ZWIFT WORKOUTS

We have two rides scheduled each week on Wednesdays: 6:05 am EST (with ride leader) and 6:45 pm EST (without ride leader).

Want to join more IRLAG Rides and Runs? Check out our upcoming socials rides through our I Race Like a Girl club feature.

ABOUT ANGELA NAETH & I RACE LIKE A GIRL

Angela Naeth is a professional triathlete, coach, and founder of IRACELIKEAGIRL - a community of girls/women who provide opportunities for females of all ages and abilities to support, encourage and inspire one another in the sport of triathlon, cycling, running and swimming. She's raced for over 10 years from Sprint to Ironman and has coached triathletes/runners/cyclists for the last 7 years. https://www.iracelikeagirl.com/

IRACELIKEAGIRL and GIRLSGETGRITTY team: https://www.iracelikeagirl.com/

name: Les supers mercredis JZQc
description:
Vous êtes invités aux Mercredis JZQc, une ride hebdomadaire conviviale, accessible et ouverte à tous.
Ambiance motivante, plaisir, respect et entraide : c’est exactement l’esprit JZQc !

🌟 Pourquoi participer ?
Une ride bien organisée, plaisante et accessible à tous.
Un moment idéal pour rouler en groupe et rencontrer d’autres membres.

📍 Parcours & leaders – Surprise chaque semaine !
• Parcours différent souvent long et plat
• Leader différent à chaque édition

⏱️ Détails
• Départ : 18 h 30
• Durée : 60 minutes
• Late Join : Activé
• Ouvert à tous

⚙️ Rythme & dynamique
🔹 Leader
Environ 2.0 à 2.2 W/kg, avec parfois de petites accélérations pour un léger défi.

🔹 Sweeper
Un rythme un peu plus lent pour accueillir ceux qui décrochent et offrir une zone plus confortable.

🔹 Liberté totale
• Suivre le leader
• Rouler avec le sweeper
• Former votre propre peloton (plus rapide ou plus lent)
• Ou rouler à votre rythme

Chaque mercredi, chacun fait sa ride à sa manière, sans pression. Le plaisir et l’inclusion avant tout.

🗣️ Communication
Un canal Discord est disponible pour ceux qui veulent discuter.
C’est totalement optionnel, avec plusieurs canaux pour faciliter les échanges.

https://discord.gg/wH2UYgGRjb

💙🟧 On vous attend !

Nouvel arrivant ou habitué, vous êtes toujours les bienvenus.
Chaque mercredi est une nouvelle belle occasion de rouler ensemble.

À mercredi ! 🚴‍♂️✨

name: RollCo: Rolling Tide - 12K
description:
🌊 ROLLING TIDE
Start small. Build big.
Welcome to Rolling Tide — RollCo’s season-long ritual race.

Every week, the distance grows by 1 km.
It begins as a short, explosive opener and steadily evolves into a full-distance endurance epic by year’s end.
This isn’t a one-off event — it’s a shared journey that rises with you.

🏁 Race Format
Rolling Tide is a categorised scratch race.
All riders start together, with categories keeping the racing fair and fast.
The only thing that changes… is the distance.

Week 1 → 1 km
Week 52 → 52 km

Early weeks reward raw power.
Later weeks demand patience, grit, and endurance.
The tide never stops rising.

Rolling Tide is about more than today’s finish line.
It’s about showing up — week after week.
Seeing the same names.
Measuring how far you’ve come as the distance stretches.

You’ll build rivalries.
You’ll track your progress.
You’ll remember the day it all began.

And by the end of the season, everyone who stayed will share the same truth:

“We rode the whole tide together.”

Why Join Rolling Collective?
We’re more than a club — we’re a global community of riders who support each other, grow together, and celebrate every ride, whether it’s a grind, a badge hunt, or a race. Join us on Discord, connect with fellow riders, and be part of something bigger.

Forward, Together

Aim for a thrilling pace of 2.7 to 3.2 w/kg average while mastering group riding skills and ride dynamics, and turbo-charge your performance with cadence, gearing, and heart rate control.
